summary: First-pass frontend-only review of Aksharaa Corporate Compliance landing page. Tested page render, all 14 required sections in order, sticky header behaviour, desktop mega menus (Services & Industries), header CTA scroll, hero counters/CTAs/floating Governance Domains card, trust strip logos, partner section, architecture cards (1-6), final CTA, footer, anchor scrolling, console errors, and mobile (390px) responsiveness including hamburger + mobile nav panel + expand/collapse + close. Overall the build is solid - zero console errors, all required data-testid attributes are present, mega menus open on hover and close on mouse-out of header, mobile nav works, counters animate to 500+/15+/6+/Pan-India, scroll-behaviour is smooth, all 6 architecture cards have 'Explore domain' links, footer carries Privacy Policy & Terms of Service plus 23 links. No bugs blocking the static phase.

issues: The small 'Pan-India Coverage' chip (absolute -right-3 -top-3 on the card) sits behind/under the card on lg viewports - only the trailing letters ('ce') are visible. Either move the chip outside the card stacking context, raise its z-index, or shift it further into the negative offsets so it is fully readable.

action_items: Minor visual polish: ensure the 'Pan-India Coverage' chip on the Hero floating card is fully visible (z-index/offset).
critical_code_review_comments: Header mega menu uses onMouseLeave on the <header> element to close - works on desktop, but the close gap between header bottom and MegaMenu top can briefly drop focus when moving the mouse onto the panel; current implementation handles it because MegaMenu is positioned `top-full` inside the header element, so it is fine. Worth a manual sanity-check after future header padding changes.

ComplianceLifecycle / ComplianceControl / EngagementModel / IntegratedWorkforce / RiskIntelligence / InstitutionalMetrics sections do not have a DOM `id` attribute. Today none of the in-page anchors target them so it is non-blocking, but adding ids (e.g. id='lifecycle') will be required as soon as you wire menu/CMS links to those sections.

Navigation Client Stories / Blog use href='#' placeholders. As called out in the request this is intentional for the static phase, but consider replacing with href='#footer' or scroll-no-op handler to avoid the page jumping to top when users click them.
Files are nicely segmented and well under 700 lines - good DRY discipline. Reveal/Counter common components are reused as intended.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend-only static React build. Public URL: https://aksh-modern.preview.emergentagent.com/. All interactive elements carry kebab-case data-testid. Mega menu testids: mega-services, mega-industries. Mobile hamburger testid: mobile-menu-open. Mobile panel testid: mobile-nav-panel. Hero counters animate from 0 - wait 2-3s before asserting final values. scroll-behavior is smooth so allow ~1s after anchor clicks before reading element rects. The Hero floating card label 'Governance Domains' is rendered uppercase via CSS (ak-kicker-bare); when asserting from JS, search for it case-insensitively. Architecture cards use testids architecture-card-01 .. architecture-card-06. Final CTA section testid is final-cta-section with primary/secondary buttons inside. Footer is <footer id='footer' data-testid='site-footer'>. Many service/industry menu links are intentional placeholders (#) per product spec - do NOT flag them as bugs.
rca_of_the_issue: No critical issues found. The single minor design issue (Pan-India chip behind hero card) is purely positional/z-index: the chip is rendered with `absolute -right-3 -top-3 hidden ... sm:block` inside the same rounded card container which has a higher visual layering. Fix is to either bump the chip's z-index above the card, move it outside the relatively-positioned card wrapper, or increase the negative offsets so it overflows clearly.
